# Getting started {#sec-getting-started}

This chapter gets you from a clone to a reproduced figure. It covers the two
reproduction tracks, the software you need, and the repository layout.

## Choose a track

| | **Track C — exhibits** *(default)* | **Track A — full rerun** |
|---|---|---|
| Starts from | the shipped aggregate layer `data/derived/` | the restricted inputs + a built companion P01 codebase |
| Requires | R and CRAN packages only | R, INLA, and the confidential data |
| Reproduces | every figure and the headline numbers | every number, from raw sources |
| Time | about a minute on a laptop | tens of minutes, plus data-access lead time |

**Start with Track C.** It ships everything it needs, needs no Census key and no
INLA, and touches no restricted data. Track A is for readers who have obtained
the confidential inputs (see `data/DATA_ACCESS.md`) and want to regenerate the
posterior from scratch.

## Requirements

- **R ≥ 4.3.** The authors ran R 4.6.0. Open the project with
  `probabilistic-childcare-deserts-replication.Rproj`, or run from the package root.
- **Track C — CRAN only.** `sf`, `dplyr`, `tidyr`, `readr`, `ggplot2`,
  `patchwork`, `scales`, `gt`, `leaflet`, `htmlwidgets`, `digest`.
- **Track A — add the modeling stack.** `spdep`, `Matrix`, `truncnorm`,
  `readxl`, `furrr`, `future`, and **INLA**. INLA is not on CRAN; install it from
  its own repository:

  ```r
  install.packages(
    "INLA",
    repos = c(INLA = "https://inla.r-inla-download.org/R/stable"),
    dep = TRUE
  )
  ```

  The authors used **INLA 26.6.8**. The full recorded environment is in
  `sessioninfo.txt`.

Source the setup helper once to check your environment and see what is installed:

```r
source("scripts/00_setup.R")
```

## Quick start (Track C)

From the package root:

```r
source("scripts/00_setup.R")            # environment check
```
```sh
Rscript scripts/reproduce_exhibits.R    # rebuild F1–F6 into results/figures/
Rscript manifest/verify_outputs.R       # check the shipped outputs against the registry
```

`reproduce_exhibits.R` reads only `data/derived/` — the disclosure-safe tract and
county tables and the public simplified tract geometry — and writes the six
figures to `results/figures/`. Compare them against the canonical
`outputs/figures/` the authors shipped. @sec-exhibits walks through each exhibit.

## Running the full pipeline (Track A)

Obtain the restricted inputs and point the environment variables at them (see
`data/DATA_ACCESS.md` and `restricted-data/README.md`):

```sh
export P01_CODEBASE_ROOT=/path/to/your/built/codebase-P01
export P07_DHR_SURVEY_DIR=/path/to/your/dhr_surveys
```

Then run the eleven steps in order, or all at once with the orchestrator:

```sh
Rscript scripts/99_reproduce_all.R      # runs steps 0.2 → 4.1, fail-closed
Rscript scripts/build_derived.R         # refresh the disclosure-safe layer
Rscript manifest/verify_outputs.R       # verify against the registry
```

Each step validates its upstream contract and stops on a failed gate. The
canonical order and the per-step outputs are in
[`_run_order.md`](../_run_order.md).

## Repository layout

```
probabilistic-childcare-deserts-replication/
├── README.md                  package overview, tracks, citation
├── _run_order.md              the canonical run order (both tracks)
├── scripts/                   the pipeline
│   ├── 00_setup.R             environment check
│   ├── 00-2 … 04-1            the eleven numbered analysis steps
│   ├── build_derived.R        custodian: protected objects → data/derived/
│   ├── reproduce_exhibits.R   Track C: rebuild figures from data/derived/
│   └── 99_reproduce_all.R     Track A: run the whole pipeline
├── R/                         function libraries (fct_io, fct_uncertainty,
│                              fct_bym2, fct_fdr, fct_viz, fct_leaflet)
├── data/
│   ├── DATA_ACCESS.md         how to obtain every input
│   └── derived/               the shipped disclosure-safe aggregate layer
├── restricted-data/           where restricted inputs go locally (git-ignored)
├── outputs/                   the authors' canonical exhibits (figures, tables,
│                              maps, key_numbers.csv)
├── results/                   Track C regeneration target
├── provenance/                source integrity + calibration metadata
├── manifest/                  SHA-256 ledger + verify_outputs.R
├── verification/              reproduction map + expected headline values
└── book/                      this guide
```

The two contracts to keep in mind as you read the code: every headline number is
written once to `outputs/key_numbers.csv` and read from there (never recomputed
downstream), and the entire `data/` tree except `data/derived/` is git-ignored so
no protected object can be committed. @sec-reproducibility returns to both.
